---
title: "quiz9"
title-block-style: none
format: live-html
engine: knitr
sidebar:
  style: docked
resources:
    - health_metrics.csv
---

{{< include /_extensions/r-wasm/live/_knitr.qmd >}}
{{< include /_extensions/r-wasm/live/_gradethis.qmd >}}

<script>
document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", () => {
  const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
  const showQ = params.get("q");

  // Show only the requested section
  if (showQ) {
    document.querySelectorAll("section[id]").forEach(section => {
      if (section.id !== showQ) section.style.display = "none";
    });
  }

  // Hide page title
  document.querySelectorAll(".quarto-title-block")
    .forEach(el => el.style.display = "none");

  // Hide ALL section titles (## ...)
  document.querySelectorAll("section[id] > h2")
    .forEach(h => h.style.display = "none");
});
</script>

## Q1 â€” One feature {#q1a}
Using the dataset `health_metrics.csv`, fit a linear regression model to predict systolic blood pressure, `sbp` using `age` as the only predictor. 

Compute the estimated intercept $\beta_{0}$.

```{webr}
df <- read.csv("health_metrics.csv")

```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
df <- read.csv("health_metrics.csv")
model <- lm(sbp ~ age, data = df)
coef(model)
# 64.295328
```

## Q1 â€” One feature {#q1b}
Using the dataset `health_metrics.csv`, fit a linear regression model to predict systolic blood pressure,`sbp` using `sodium` as the only predictor. 

Compute the estimated slope $\beta_{1}$.

```{webr}
df <- read.csv("health_metrics.csv")

```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
df <- read.csv("health_metrics.csv")
model <- lm(sbp ~ sodium, data = df)
coef(model)
# 0.04144923 
```

## Q2 â€” Predict {#q2a}
Using the dataset `health_metrics.csv`, predict the systolic blood pressure for when $\text{sodium} = 2000$. 

Hint: use `predict` or use $\beta_{0}$ and $\beta_{1}$ to calculate it by hand.
```{webr}
df <- read.csv("health_metrics.csv")

```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
df <- read.csv("health_metrics.csv")
model <- lm(sbp ~ sodium, data = df)
predict(model, newdata = data.frame(sodium=2000))
# 115.2822 
```

## Q2 â€” Predict {#q2b}
Using the dataset `health_metrics.csv`, predict the diabetes progression for when $\text{bmi} = 23$. 

Hint: use `predict` or use $\beta_{0}$ and $\beta_{1}$ to calculate it by hand.
```{webr}
df <- read.csv("health_metrics.csv")

```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
df <- read.csv("health_metrics.csv")
model <- lm(sbp ~ bmi, data = df)
predict(model, newdata = data.frame(bmi = 23))
# 113.9065
```

## Q3 - Check normality {#q3a}
Using `health_metrics.csv`, fit a linear regression model predicting `sbp` from `sodium`. 
Produce a QQ plot of the residuals and determine whether the normality assumption holds.

Hint: Use `qqnorm` and `qqline`.
```{webr}
df <- read.csv("health_metrics.csv")

```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
df <- read.csv("health_metrics.csv")
model <- lm(sbp ~ sodium, data = df)
qqnorm(resid(model))
qqline(resid(model))

# yes normality assumption holds!
```

## Q3 - Check normality {#q3b}
Using `health_metrics.csv`, fit a linear regression model predicting `sbp` from `hr`. 
Produce a QQ plot of the residuals and determine whether the normality assumption holds.

Hint: Use `qqnorm` and `qqline`.
```{webr}
df <- read.csv("health_metrics.csv")

```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
df <- read.csv("health_metrics.csv")
model <- lm(sbp ~ hr, data = df)
qqnorm(resid(model))
qqline(resid(model))

# normality does not hold

```

## Q4 - Compare predictions {#q4a}
Using `health_metrics.csv`, fit a linear regression model predicting `sbp` from `bmi`. 
What is the difference in predicted `sbp` between a patient whose `bmi` is 25 and a patient with a `bmi` of 20?
```{webr}
df <- read.csv("health_metrics.csv")

```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
df <- read.csv("health_metrics.csv")
model <- lm(sbp ~ bmi, data = df)
bmi20 <- predict(model, newdata = data.frame(bmi = 20))
bmi25 <- predict(model, newdata = data.frame(bmi = 25))
bmi25 - bmi20

# 24.61191
```

## Q4 - Compare predictions {#q4b}
Using `health_metrics.csv`, fit a linear regression model predicting `sbp` from `age`. 
What is the difference in predicted `sbp` between a patient whose aged 60 and a patient aged 40?
```{webr}
df <- read.csv("health_metrics.csv")

```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
df <- read.csv("health_metrics.csv")
model <- lm(sbp ~ age, data = df)
age40 <- predict(model, newdata = data.frame(age = 40))
age60 <- predict(model, newdata = data.frame(age = 60))
age60 - age40

# 29.07604
```
